sections.title: Mission Alignment
sections.promptHint: 1–2 paragraphs connecting these OKRs to the company mission, strategy, or prior quarter's results. Answer: why these objectives, why now, and how they ladder up to what matters most.
sections.ragQuery: company mission strategy priorities previous quarter goals

sections.title: Objectives & Key Results
sections.promptHint: 3–5 Objectives. Each Objective should be: qualitative, inspiring, and slightly uncomfortable (a stretch goal). Under each Objective, list 3–5 Key Results. Each KR must be: quantitative, specific, time-bound, and binary (hit or miss). Include a 1–2 sentence rationale for each Objective. Format: ## Objective: [text], then numbered KRs below it.
sections.ragQuery: goals objectives metrics targets KPIs performance priorities

sections.title: Dependencies
sections.promptHint: List cross-team or external dependencies that could block KR achievement. For each: the KR affected, the dependency, who owns it, and the risk level if it is not resolved.
sections.ragQuery: dependencies blockers cross-team collaboration external constraints

sections.title: Tracking Cadence
sections.promptHint: Describe how OKRs will be tracked: weekly check-in format, monthly scoring ritual, end-of-quarter review process, and who is responsible for keeping the OKR document updated.
